items.question: Cause: Model trained on data ending 2024-06. Effect when asked about a 2025 regulation?
items.answer: May omit, guess, or hallucinate details about the 2025 regulation unless you supply authoritative text or a retrieval tool fetches it.

items.question: Cause: You attach current docs to the prompt. Effect on cutoff limitation?
items.answer: The model can answer about those facts **for this request** because they appear in context, even if absent from pretraining.

items.question: What if you ask Claude for today's stock price without tools or pasted data?
items.answer: The model may guess, cite stale training-era prices, or hedge—because live market data is after cutoff and not in context. Fix: enable a market data tool or paste the quote with timestamp.

items.question: What if you upgrade production from an older model ID to a newer one with a later cutoff?
items.answer: Time-sensitive answers may improve for post-cutoff facts, but behavior and hallucination rates can shift—re-run evals and update user-facing disclaimers instead of assuming identical outputs.
